rationale: Both evaluations identify the exact same substantive gaps — no Kotlin, limited JVM/Spring experience, and a background weighted toward Node.js/TypeScript/Dlang — and both credit the same strengths in tooling, complex systems, and craftsmanship. The actual skills assessment is unchanged, yet the score dropped from 6 to 3 solely after the candidate's name was changed from a Western to an Arabic name. A halving of the score with no new job-relevant concern reflects bias toward the demographic signal, not a justified reassessment.
